'''Luke Keary''' (born 3 February 1992) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|FE}} or {{rlp|HB}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|Ireland}} at international level.

Keary previously played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], with whom he won the [[2014 NRL Grand Final|2014 NRL Premiership]], and the [[Sydney Roosters]] where he won the [[2018 NRL Grand Final|2018]] and [[2019 NRL Grand Final|2019 NRL Premierships]] and was a fill-in captain. Keary won the [[Clive Churchill Medal]] in the [[2018 NRL Grand Final|2018]] with the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]].

==Early career==
Keary was born in [[Ipswich, Queensland]], [[Australia]], and is of [[Irish New Zealanders|Irish]] descent<ref>{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/34923753/7th-tackle-luke-keary-in-line-for-world-cup-gig-with-ireland/ |title=Luke Keary in line for World Cup gig with Ireland |publisher=Yahoo |date=6 April 2017 |access-date=6 April 2017}}</ref> and was raised in the suburb of [[Raceview, Queensland|Raceview]] and attended St. Mary's Primary School in Ipswich.

He played junior rugby league for Ipswich Brothers. At age 10, Keary moved to [[Sydney]] with his family.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/rabbitohs-half-luke-keary-is-the-mouse-that-roared-as-south-sydney-brace-for-big-test/story-fniabob6-1227070643083|title=Rabbitohs Half Luke Keary is the Mouse That Roared As South Sydney Brace for Big Test|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> In Sydney, Keary played junior rugby league for the Kellyville Bushrangers and later [[Hills District Bulls]] and attended [[Oakhill College]] in [[Castle Hill, New South Wales|Castle Hill]].<ref name="autogenerated1">{{cite web|url=http://www.oakhill.nsw.edu.au/resource/OakhillDriveV24(web).pdf|title=The Oakhill Drive|date=May 2012|website=Oakhill.nsw.edu.au|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> In 2010, Keary represented the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby union team]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://austschools.rugbynet.com.au/default.asp?id=170225|title=RugbyNet – Australian Schools Rugby Union|website=Austschools.rugbynet.com.au|access-date=2015-12-23|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151224052840/http://austschools.rugbynet.com.au/default.asp?id=170225|archive-date=24 December 2015}}</ref> That same year he represented the New South Wales Combined Independent Schools (NSWCIS) rugby league team at the Under 18s National Championships.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=152044|title=2010–18 Years Burleigh Heads – Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League – FOX SPORTS PULSE|website=Sportingpulse.com|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com.au/nswcis.php|title=NSWCIS Rugby League News and heaps of stuff|website=Ourfootyteam.com.au|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> In 2011, Keary returned to Queensland, moving to the [[Gold Coast, Queensland|Gold Coast]] where he attended [[Griffith University]] and played a season with the [[Burleigh Bears]], playing for their FOGS Colts and [[Queensland Cup]] teams.<ref name="autogenerated1"/> At the end of the year he won Burleigh's FOGS Colts Best Back Award.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.qrl.com.au/?s=article-display&id=44225 |title=Official Site of the Queensland Rugby League – QRL |website=qrl.com.au |access-date=15 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010055129/http://www.qrl.com.au/?s=article-display&id=44225 |archive-date=10 October 2017 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

Growing up, Keary supported the [[Brisbane Broncos]], idolising [[Allan Langer]] who also hails from Ipswich, and stated his ambitions of one day representing them before signing with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] ahead of the [[2012 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/news/article/-/24686398/keary-set-to-face-former-beloved-broncos/ |title=Keary set to face former beloved Broncos Yahoo!7 Sport |access-date=2016-02-04 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20140812041552/https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/news/article/-/24686398/keary-set-to-face-former-beloved-broncos/ |archive-date=12 August 2014 }}</ref> Keary played for the Rabbitohs' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2012, captaining the team and playing 24 games, in which he scored 20 tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Luke-Keary_3604/Matches/|title=Luke Keary|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151224104034/http://rleague.com/players/Luke-Keary_3604/Matches/|archive-date=24 December 2015 }}</ref> At the end of season, he was named on the bench in the 2012 NYC Team of the Year and won South Sydney's NYC Best and Fairest award.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/69388/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=National Rugby League|date=21 August 2012|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref>
[[File:lukekeary.jpg|thumb|right|Keary playing for the [[North Sydney Bears]] in 2012]]

==Playing career==
===2013===
In January 2013, Keary was a member of the Queensland Junior Emerging Origin squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://forums.leagueunlimited.com/showthread.php?t=413566|title=McQueen and Keary Named in Emerging Maroons Squads – The Front Row Forum :: Rugby League|website=Forums.leagueunlimited.com|date=2012-12-14|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> In Round 12 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Keary made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] against the [[Newcastle Knights]] off the interchange bench in the clubs 25–18 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-beat-newcastle-knights-25-18-in-nrl-round-12/story-fni3gki8-1226655052041|title=Rabbitohs Beat Knights 25-18 in NRL Round 12|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> In Round 20 against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], Keary scored his first and second tries NRL career tries off the interchange bench in South Sydney's 32–4 win at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/south-sydney-rabbitohs-blow-gold-coast-titans-away-to-get-nrl-premiership-tilt-back-on-track/story-fn2mcuj6-1226686862728|title=Rabbitohs Blow Titans Away to Get NRL Premiership Back on Track|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> 
On 22 August 2013, Keary extended his contract with the South Sydney Rabbitohs to the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-re-sign-young-gun-luke-keary-until-end-of-2015-nrl-season/story-fni3gki8-1226701160739|title=Rabbitohs Re-Sign Young Gun Luke Leary Until End of 2015|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-23 |date=21 August 2013}}</ref> Keary finished his debut year with two tries in 10 matches.

===2014===
In February, Keary was selected for the Rabbitohs inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad. Keary was preparing his breakout year but it was ruined early due to a torn pectoral muscle injury in the Nines.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2015-12-23|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rabbitoh-luke-keary-to-miss-bulk-of-2014-nrl-season-following-surgery/story-e6frf3uu-1226830600092|title=Rabbitoh Luke Leary to Miss Bulk of 2014 NRL Season Following Surgery|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> Keary made his return to the South Sydney team in Round 17 against the Gold Coast Titans, scoring a try in the clubs 14-10 shock loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/winning-ugly-just-fine-for-desperate-titans-as-they-beat-south-sydney/story-fnca0von-1226981048094|title=Winning Ugly Just Fine for Desperate Titans As They Beat South Sydney|publisher=The Australian|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> On 5 October, in the [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]], Keary played at [[Rugby league positions#Five-eighth|five-eighth]] in the 30–6 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/story-fni3fbgz-1227080755667|title=Rabbitohs Win 2014 NRL Grand Final|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-23|date=5 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/rabbitohs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82375/default.aspx|title=Rabbitohs Grand Final Player Ratings|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2014-10-05|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> Keary finished the [[2014 NRL season|season]] with 3 tries from 12 matches.

===2015===
On 27 January, Keary re-signed with the South Sydney club on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=Brad Walter|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/south-sydney-rabbitohs-resign-adam-reynolds-and-luke-keary-20150127-12zdn1.html|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs re-sign Adam Reynolds and Luke Keary|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2015-01-27|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> On 23 February, he played for the Rabbitohs in the [[2015 World Club Series|2015 World Club Challenge]] match against [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] premiers [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], playing at five-eighth and scoring a try in the Rabbitohs' 39–0 win at [[Langtree Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/world-club-challenge-south-sydney-rabbitohs-thrash-st-helens-saints-to-complete-aussie-series-sweep/story-e6frf3uu-1227234753346|title=Rabbitohs Beat Knights 25-18 in NRL Round 12|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-23|date=22 February 2015}}</ref> In April, he was selected for the [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] team for the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] match, but withdrew due to a foot injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-quiet-on-injury-that-ruled-luke-keary-out-of-city-origin-side/story-fniabob6-1227323587439|title=South Sydney Quite on Injury That Ruled Luke Keary Out of City of Origin Side|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref> He finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in 24 matches and scoring 4 tries for South Sydney.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTItMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjAxNDktLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-23|access-date=2015-12-26}}</ref>

===2016===
In January, Keary was involved in a confrontation with actor and Rabbitohs club co-owner [[Russell Crowe]] in a pre-season bonding session at Crowe's farm in [[Nana Glen, New South Wales]]. It was revealed that Keary was furious after Crowe berated him, [[Cameron McInnes]] and some of the younger players. When Keary defended them, Crowe told him that he was not worth the money of his contract, sending Keary into a rage, first at Crowe and then at senior Souths player [[Sam Burgess]] for not sticking up for the younger players. Keary was ordered by Crowe to leave the property, which he did, catching a taxi at about 3:00am.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/what-russell-crowe-really-said-to-luke-keary-to-send-him-storming-out-of-rabbitohs-team-bonding-session/news-story/89a16270d708e3c4538d389396c46a8b|url-access=subscription|archive-url=https://archive.today/20230507130934/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/what-russell-crowe-really-said-to-luke-keary-to-send-him-storming-out-of-rabbitohs-team-bonding-session/news-story/89a16270d708e3c4538d389396c46a8b?amp&nk=10459aa48dcb4f0610ea9f9d67d916f2-1683464985|archive-date=May 7, 2023|date=January 14, 2016|title=What Russell Crowe really said to Luke Keary to send him storming out of Rabbitohs team bonding session|first=James|last=Hooper|work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/jan/20/south-sydney-owner-russell-crowe-apologises-for-luke-keary-tirade|title=South Sydney owner Russell Crowe apologises for Luke Keary tirade|agency=Australian Associated Press|date=20 January 2016|website=The Guardian|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref>

At the start of the year, Keary, who was off contract at season's end, was linked to sign with the [[Sydney Roosters]], the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] who offered him a contract worth $1.2 million over three-years, or even a shift to [[Rugby Union]] for 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/rabbitohs/nrl-2016-south-sydney-rabbitohs-fiveeighth-luke-keary-considers-switching-to-rugby-union/news-story/f0420a79f031474d231abc2346a662b4|title=NRL 2016: Rabbitohs star Luke Keary considers rugby union|date=8 March 2016|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/st-george-illawarra-dragons-weigh-up-luke-kearys-12-million-contract-request-20160609-gpfp9u.html|title=St George Illawarra Dragons weigh up Luke Keary's $1.2 million contract request|first=Michael|last=Chammas|date=10 June 2016|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref> On 21 June, Keary announced that he signed a 2-year contract with the Roosters starting in 2017.<ref>{{Cite web | url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/rabbitohs/reports-south-sydney-playmaker-luke-keary-has-signed-twoyear-roosters-deal/news-story/7b520abead4f4930f2b20e97a43049b4 | title=How Robbo snared Keary from under Dragons| date=20 June 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/south-sydney-star-luke-keary-signs-with-sydney-roosters-until-2018/news-story/8800d13f75742e01bfcf9005e6125b2a|title=South Sydney star Luke Keary signs with Sydney Roosters until 2018|date=21 June 2016|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref> In Round 21 against the [[Canberra Raiders]], Keary would play his last match for the South Sydney Rabbitohs after he suffered a season ending [[hamstring]] injury in the woeful 54–4 loss at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-07-31/canberra-raiders-in-nrl-top-three-after-beating-south-sydney/7676120|title=Raiders in top three after 54-4 rout of Rabbitohs|publisher=[[ABC News (Australia)|ABC News]]|location=Australia|date=31 July 2016|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref> Keary finished his last season with the South Sydney club playing in 17 matches and scoring three tries in the [[2016 NRL season]].

===2017===
In Round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Keary made his club debut for Sydney Roosters against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], having a successful match by scoring 2 tries in the 32–18 win at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-gold-coast-titans-v-sydney-roosters-at-cbus-super-stadium/news-story/ae2ad9af95df5f906216f2849f66260b|title=NRL scores: Luke Keary, Mitchell Pearce lead Roosters win over Titans|date=4 March 2017|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref>
Keary made 26 appearances for Sydney Roosters and was part of the side which made it all the way to the preliminary final before suffering a shock 16–29 defeat by a depleted [[North Queensland Cowboys]] team.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/sep/23/sydney-roosters-v-north-queensland-cowboys-nrl-preliminary-final-live|title=Cowboys beat Roosters in NRL preliminary final – as it happened|first1=Sam|last1=Perry|newspaper=The Guardian|date=23 September 2017|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTEtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMjAxNDktLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List – Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref>

At season's end, Keary contemplated playing in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]]. He later said, "I stuck my hand up to play for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in 2017. It didn’t eventuate, I had a few [injuries] at the back end of the year. I’ve always had a connection there since I was a kid. Dad’s family is still over there. I haven’t been over there but you just feel proud whenever he talked about it. It makes me feel good thinking about it."<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/keary-to-answer-ireland-s-call-as-suaalii-targets-kangaroos-world-cup-berth-20220805-p5b7ix.html|title=Keary to answer Ireland's call as Suaalii targets Kangaroos World Cup berth|author=Dan Walsh}}</ref>

===2018===
In 2018, Keary suffered a broken jaw at pre season training and missed over a month of training and trial games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2018-broncos-darius-boyd-boost-perth-expansion-ramps-up/news-story/58f2146a5c0cfbde040f732a591ddeb0|title=NRL 2018: Roosters star Luke Keary broken jaw at pre-season training|date=31 January 2018|access-date=8 July 2018}}</ref>

Keary received the Clive Churchill medal in the 2018 Grand Final where his team the Sydney Roosters beat the Melbourne Storm 21–6.

He capped a stellar season by earning selection in the [[Australian Kangaroos]] team as starting five-eighth for Test matches against New Zealand and Tonga.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/09/new-zealand-v-australia-team-lists-mens-womens-junior-sides/|title=New Zealand v Australia team lists: Men's, women's, junior sides|date=13 October 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
Keary started the [[2019 NRL season]] in good form as the club won 7 of their first 9 games.  Keary's good form meant that he was in line to be selected for New South Wales in the [[2019 State of Origin series]].  In Round 11 against Newcastle, Keary was taken from the field with concussion and was subsequently ruled out for 6 weeks.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/luke-keary-to-miss-up-to-six-weeks-leaving-sydney-roosters-short-of-halves-options/news-story/94a245d74131ae4a6c237f7a900b7ee6/|title=Luke Keary to miss up to six weeks leaving Sydney Roosters short of halves options|work=Fox Sports|date=2 June 2019}}</ref>

On 15 August, Keary was spoken about by Brisbane coach [[Anthony Seibold]] comparing him to [[Darius Boyd]] saying "I had a look through the competition last weekend, there is an Australian No.6 (Keary) playing for a team in Sydney who only made four metres last week and missed five tackles, Not one thing was said in the media about that. His team won and our team won.  For whatever reason because Darius only had one run, he has been crucified all week from what I understand.  I just think everyone needs to back off on Darius".  Keary spoke to the media in response saying "I don’t really care to be honest. It’s a little bit odd. I don’t know him, I’ve never spoke to him. I don’t care what he says. I don’t care what he thinks".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/roosters-star-luke-keary-hits-back-at-anthony-seibolds-bizarre-outburst-about-his-form/news-story/528d723636d3395194f9b02a9673d549/|title=Roosters star Luke Keary hits back at Anthony Seibold's bizarre outburst about his form|work=Fox Sports|date=15 August 2019}}</ref>

Keary played at five-eighth for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] in which the club won their second consecutive premiership defeating Canberra 14–8 in the decider.  It was Keary's third premiership victory as a player.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|publisher=BBC Sport }}</ref>

On 7 October, Keary was named for the Australian side for the upcoming [[2019 Oceania Cup (rugby league)|Oceania Cup]] fixtures.

===2020===
In round 9 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Keary scored two tries as the Sydney Roosters defeated North Queensland 42–16 at [[Queensland Country Bank Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/07/09/ikuvalu-scores-five-as-roosters-rout-cowboys/|title=Ikuvalu scores five as roosters rout north queensland|publisher=National Rugby League|date=9 July 2020}}</ref>
In August 2020, Keary extended his contract with the Sydney Roosters until the end of the 2024 season. In round 18 against Newcastle, he scored two tries as the Sydney Roosters won the match 42–12 at the [[Sydney Cricket Ground]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/tupou-scores-100th-nrl-try-120127722.html?guccounter=1&guce_referrer=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ29vZ2xlLmNvbS5hdS8&guce_referrer_sig=AQAAAMqhFY6Ameown1_HyJlxuwQAa8fWlP9bGFwbEZqVjGKUptjRAJkeKqKjnc3wxo0k8fV5HMhefysxC-VgezY9hf1wkrfkUblsbL-XqPpDQ9Kmq0NscB79c14_pVX5EqEjJ13TVzDCiyUy4axkVKfBj8p0OnVHGJLcQJQjvlDuQRmZ/|title=Tupou scores 100th NRL try as Roosters end Knights' top-four hopes|website=uk.sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>

The following week, he scored a further two tries in the club's 34–18 victory over Cronulla-Sutherland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-09-19/nrl-sydney-roosters-beat-sharks-melbourne-storm-gold-coast-win/12681786/|title=Sydney Roosters defeat Sharks ahead of NRL finals, Shaun Johnson suffers serious injury|publisher=ABC News|location=Australia|date=19 September 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
Following the Roosters decision to release halfback [[Kyle Flanagan (rugby league)|Kyle Flanagan]], Keary was moved to halfback by the Roosters. 

In round 3 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Keary suffered a season ending [[ACL injury]] in the [[Sydney Roosters]] 26–16 loss against archrivals South Sydney.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-sydney-roosters-cant-win-after-luke-keary-injury-rabbitohs-loss-braith-anasta-gorden-tallis-say/news-story/0183f1f7313367d11a57a740359d7389/|title='Earth-shattering': Legends say Roosters can 'kiss season goodbye' after 'catastrophic' blow|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=26 March 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
In Round 1, Keary returned from his ACL injury, in a 6-20 loss to the Newcastle Knights at the [[Sydney Cricket Ground]].

In Round 14, Keary suffered a concussion v the Melbourne Storm, leaving the field 30 minutes into the match and not returning.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/nrl-2022-luke-keary-concussion-sydney-roosters-news-return-medical-retirement-latest-boyd-cordner/news-story/ccf23b7bb76ed17ba87c00571d3f92c0|title=‘How is that dramatic?’: Kent, Buzz lock horns over Keary’s ‘big decision’ amid concussion hell|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=30 June 2022 }}</ref>

In Round 19, Keary returned from his concussion, and moved back to five-eighth with Sam Walker taking over at halfback. Keary scored his first try in almost 2 years in a 42-12 victory over the Knights at [[McDonald Jones Stadium]]
Keary made 22 appearances for the Sydney Roosters in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished sixth on the table. Keary played in the Sydney Roosters elimination final loss to arch-rivals [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-sydney-roosters-season-review-ins-and-outs-team-news-trent-robinson-matt-lodge-joseph-sualii/news-story/382f8d30c3ace5024a0c4f25d1920153/|title=Robbo switch that woke sleeping giant as full extent of cap crunch laid bare: Brutal Review|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=12 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
In round 5 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Keary scored two tries for the Sydney Roosters in their 28–20 victory over Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-bailey-simonsson-sin-bin-james-tedesco-tackle-roosters-vs-eels-match-review-committee/news-story/485f1e59cface9ea54faf524a4e6d296/|title='Ropeable at the time': Eels flyer slapped with hefty ban for ugly Tedesco hit|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=30 March 2023 }}</ref>
In round 8, Keary kicked the winning field goal in the Sydney Roosters 27–26 victory over St. George Illawarra in the ANZAC Day game.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-sydney-roosters-v-st-george-illawarra-victor-radley-sin-bin-controversial-james-tedesco/news-story/a3732ba186ac731fe7015c41a1172f32/|title=Victor Radley learns fate after Rooster enforcer unloads over 's***' Anzac Day sin bin call|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=25 April 2023 }}</ref>
In round 12, Keary scored two tries for the Sydney Roosters in their 24-22 loss against St. George Illawarra at [[Kogarah Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-05-19/nrl-dragons-roosters-rabbitohs-eels/102359064/|title=Eels upset Rabbitohs 36-16 to snap losing NRL run, Dragons snatch 24-22 victory over Roosters|website=www.abc.net.au|date=19 May 2023 }}</ref>
Keary played 26 matches for the Sydney Roosters in the 2023 NRL season as the club finished 7th on the table and qualified for the finals. Keary played in both of the clubs finals games as they were eliminated in the second week against Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/sydney-roosters-nrl-2023-season-review/88b2453a9bca06123c73704b#:~:text=While%20their%20late%2Dseason%20heroics,to%20reach%20the%20prelim%20finals./|title=NRL 2023: Sydney Roosters season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=17 September 2023 }}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
On 29 April, it was announced that Keary was expected to announce his decision to retire from the NRL at the end of the 2024 season, later in the afternoon Keary announced his official retirement from the game after 12 seasons in the top grade.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2024-04-29 |title=Roosters star Keary makes shock decision to retire |url=https://www.theage.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-star-keary-makes-shock-decision-to-retire-20240426-p5fmv5.html |access-date=2024-04-29 |website=The Age |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-04-29 |title=Luke Keary announces his retirement |url=https://www.roosters.com.au/news/2024/04/29/luke-keary-announces-his-retirement/?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAAR06FpT98o7I3XTr_Xukfk8yyi86QFY6uRIxvU78imNltKRXOVhcIsB16ws_aem_AafvFFD9Vmc_VoERoqDIdOCc48-3oUa3NnuMvUkEkXX15KVZ0PpwX79R4YXKENY6qni4hJECwTatXMMCxMeGi9dp |access-date=2024-04-29 |website=Sydney Roosters |language=en}}</ref>
On 17 June, Keary announced he had changed his mind about retirement signing a deal to join the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the Super League starting in 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-sydney-roosters-luke-keary-backflip-catalans-dragons-super-league-2025-squad-salary-cap-brandon-smith/news-story/830497946a65e0f167a9766d18f8e8a9/|title=Exclusive: Keary's double backflip as Roosters playmaker signs with Super League club|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=17 June 2024 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Betfred Super League|date=12 Jul 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=DRAGONS SIGN KEARY FOR 2025|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4362/dragons-sign-keary-for-2025}}</ref>
Keary played a total of 26 games for the Sydney Roosters in the 2024 NRL season as they finished third and qualified for the finals. Keary played in the clubs preliminary final loss against Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-sydney-roosters-season-review-the-mole-analysis-20241001-p5kesz.html/|title=The Mole's end of season review: Maligned signing suddenly looms as Roosters saviour after exodus, injuries|website=www.nine.com.au|date=30 September 2024 }}</ref>

===2025===
In round 1 of the [[2025 Super League season]], Keary made his club debut for Catalans in their upset loss against [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20p9z9lvg7o|title=Two tries for Cust as new-look Hull FC beat Catalans|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 2 May, Keary came under fire for comments he made about the Super League competition. In an interview with Wide World of Sports, Keary said "If [the NRL] don't buy it, they're in a lot of trouble. All the players are open to it," said Keary, who switched to play from Australia to Ireland in 2022. "[The Super League] is in such a bad way, the game is horrendous over here. It's the product, the coverage, the news around the game, there is zero. You watch the games, they're near unwatchable. The coverage and everything, and because we're in French, they don't show the games. One thing I've appreciated over here, seeing the state of the Super League [is] how good of a place [the NRL] is in. They're literally on their knees begging for the NRL [to buy in]. The players all want it". Rugby League Commercial managing director Rhodri Jones later came out saying that Keary's comments were "over-dramatic".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5y4w5wn8nlo|title=Jones defends Super League after Keary criticism|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Keary played 27 matches for Catalans in the 2025 Super League season as the club missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/luke-keary-admits-embarrassing-year-as-a-catalans-dragons-player-after-reflecting-on-early-season-gaffe/|title=Luke Keary admits ’embarrassing’ year as a Catalans Dragons player and early-season ‘gaffe’|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

== Representative career ==
Keary's [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] eligibility was a point of discussion within the media due to his being born and raised, for the first 10 years of his life, in [[Ipswich, Queensland]]. He spent the following eight years in [[Sydney]], before returning to Queensland for another two years. In 2014, Keary wrote to then [[National Rugby League|NRL]] CEO Dave Smith requesting that he be considered a Queenslander for future representative opportunities.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-05-06/qrl-backs-origin-eligibility-review/5434224|title=Queensland Rugby League chairman backs Mal Meninga's call for State of Origin eligibility review|website=ABC|date=6 May 2014}}</ref> The request was denied and the 2012 eligibility rules were upheld.

Keary made his international debut for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in 2018 against New Zealand.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/keary-shakes-off-concussion-to-leave-tongans-with-headache-20181019-p50aq0.html|title=Keary shakes off concussion to leave Tongans with headache|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=19 October 2018}}</ref> In 2020, he was selected to make his [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] debut for New South Wales in Game 1.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/i-m-very-comfortable-with-nsw-keary-laughs-off-maroons-claim-20201029-p569ui.html|title='I'm very comfortable with NSW': Keary laughs off Maroons claim|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=29 October 2020}}</ref>

Following New South Wales upset loss in Game 1 of the [[2020 State of Origin series]] against Queensland, Keary was cut from the New South Wales squad for Game 2 and was not selected for Game 3 as the Blues suffered a shock 2–1 series defeat, with his "successor" at South Sydney, [[Cody Walker (rugby league)|Cody Walker]] playing Games 2 & 3 in the 6 jersey.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2020/nov/18/state-of-origin-game-3-qld-maroons-v-nsw-blues-live-scores-queensland-teams-results-kick-off-start-time-2020-series-decider-dates-news-updates-suncorp-stadium/|title=State of Origin 2020 game 3: Qld Maroons beat NSW Blues – as it happened|newspaper=The Guardian|date=18 November 2020|last1=Howcroft|first1=Jonathan}}</ref>
On 16 October 2022, Keary made his debut for Ireland at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] and earned man of the match honours in their 48–2 victory over Jamaica.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63277983|title=Rugby League World Cup: Ireland 48-2 Jamaica – Wolfhounds score 10 tries in emphatic win|publisher=BBC Sport }}</ref>
